news.title: Brigade Group & Bain Capital to jointly develop two million sq ft project in Bengaluru
news.para: Brigade Group and Bain Capital are joining forces for a major development in Whitefield, Bengaluru. This 50:50 joint venture will invest approximately ₹2,200 crore. The project will span 11 acres, featuring two million sq ft of grade-A office space and a five-star hotel. It recently acquired land in Osman Nagar, Hyderabad for a residential project.

news.title: Mindspace Business Parks REIT's net operating income up 29.2% in FY26
news.para: Mindspace REIT announces 29.2 percent growth in net operating income for the financial year 2025-26. The company's board has approved raising funds up to ₹15,700 crore. A distribution of ₹6.64 per unit has been declared for the quarter ending March 31, 2026.

news.title: ETRealty Awards 2026 winner Open Greens sets a new benchmark for future-ready workspaces
news.para: Open Greens in Noida has been honored with two ET Realty Awards for its innovative commercial design. Nestled in Sector 136, this development boasts eco-friendly architecture and cutting-edge infrastructure, ensuring seamless access for businesses.

news.title: How MahaRERA's circular 50/2025 changes the cancellation deadlock and the questions it leaves behind
news.para: A recent Bombay High Court ruling and a new MahaRERA circular provide a crucial solution for developers facing payment defaults. Previously, stalled property titles were a major issue. Now, MahaRERA can appoint an officer to execute cancellation deeds on behalf of non-compliant allottees. This streamlines the process, allowing developers to clear encumbrances and improve project cash flow.

news.title: Neo Alternative Asset Managers commits ₹300 crore to Hyderabad residential project
news.para: Neo Alternative Asset Managers has committed ₹300 crore in structured debt to a premium residential project in Hyderabad’s Narsingi, marking its first real estate investment in the city and underscoring growing institutional interest in West Hyderabad.

news.title: Why does wall putty crack and how can you prevent it?
news.para: Birla White Wall Care Putty doesn't just make walls smooth; it actually makes them stronger.  Cracks usually happen because of bad application, dampness, or cheap materials. Using the right method and a good product, like Birla White WallCare Putty, is key.  For walls that stay smooth and crack-free for ages, pick a reliable, high-quality putty.

news.title: Strata exits Bengaluru industrial asset for ₹35 crore
news.para: Strata has sold an industrial asset in Bengaluru for ₹35.3 crore, delivering a 15.3% internal rate of return over five years. The transaction reflects continued investor interest in income-generating industrial assets with long-term leases.

news.title: Omaxe to invest ₹250 crore in residential project in Lucknow; Brigade acquires 5.72-acre land in Hyderabad
news.para: Omaxe Garv Buildtech will invest ₹250 crore in a new residential project in Lucknow. The Omaxe Cassia project will offer 2BHK and 3BHK apartments. Meanwhile, Brigade Enterprises has bought land in Hyderabad. They plan to build a residential project on this site.

news.title: Belagavi civic body cancels PIDs of 31 unauthorised buildings, probes official nexus
news.para: Belagavi City Corporation has cancelled Property Identification Numbers for 31 unauthorized buildings. These buildings were constructed on lands bought through Rs 100 stamp paper agreements without valid land records. An inquiry found the properties were in a drain buffer zone. Officials involved in the irregular PID issuance are now facing an inquiry.

news.title: Delhi submits draft climate action plan for approval
news.para: Delhi's environment department has submitted a draft State Action Plan on Climate Change (SPACC) 2.0 for Union government approval. This updated plan incorporates recent climate science and data, defining sector-specific actions with measurable targets up to 2030. It aims to strengthen governance, integrate nature-based solutions, and align with national and global climate commitments.

news.title: Maharashtra government moves closer to seal Air India building buyout after ₹1,600 crore fund transfer
news.para: Maharashtra government is set to acquire the iconic Air India building in Mumbai. The Finance Department has transferred Rs 1,600 crore to the Public Works Department. The agreement is expected to be signed within two weeks. The state plans to renovate the 23-storey structure and shift government offices there.

news.title: SC orders Larsen & Toubro to vacate ₹1,000 crore Pali Hill bungalow
news.para: After a two-decade legal battle, Larsen & Toubro has been ordered by the Supreme Court to vacate a prime Pali Hill bungalow worth over Rs 1,000 crore. The company's attempt to claim co-ownership by purchasing shares during eviction proceedings was rejected by courts as a strategy to block possession. The final ruling upholds the owners' right to reclaim the property.

news.title: Soon high-rises on nine-metres roads across Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai's BMC is easing building height restrictions, allowing skyscrapers on narrow roads. An amendment to DCPR 2034 introduces graded height permissions based on road width, potentially unlocking redevelopment for thousands of old buildings. While boosting urban renewal, concerns remain about infrastructure strain and emergency access.

news.title: Haryana set to make EV charging points mandatory in societies, malls & offices
news.para: Haryana's Building Code is being amended to mandate EV charging infrastructure in new residential and non-residential projects. This move aims to accelerate electric vehicle adoption by ensuring charging facilities are integrated from the planning stage, with exemptions from Floor Area Ratio calculations to encourage developer participation.

news.title: Delhi development body put 142 major plots on sales to generate revenue
news.para: The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) is boosting revenue by e-auctioning over 140 diverse land sites and plots for residential, industrial, commercial, and institutional uses. This initiative aims to monetize vacant land and assets, with registrations open until May 22 for properties across prime Delhi locations.

news.title: Haryana to resume stilt-plus-four, ban limited to Gurugram
news.para: Haryana government is set to allow 'stilt-plus-four' floor constructions in most districts after a court clarified its recent ban was specific to Gurugram. This decision revives hopes for developers and homeowners, with final approval pending the certified court order. The policy, however, will still require neighbor NOCs and plots facing 10m wide roads.

news.title: Over 28,000 property owners in Nagpur have not paid tax even once
news.para: Nagpur Municipal Corporation faces a significant financial lapse as 28,398 property owners owe Rs259.48 crore in unpaid property tax, with some defaulting since construction or purchase. This widespread failure in tax assessment and recovery, particularly in Ashi Nagar zone, highlights systemic loopholes and impacts civic amenities due to fund constraints.

news.title: Mumbai: Dissidents seek report on SGM granting Taximen’s colony redevelopment to Syba Spaces
news.para: Dissent brews in Bombay Taximen’s Cooperative Housing Society after a special meeting granted redevelopment rights to Syba Spaces. Opposing members allege procedural irregularities, lack of transparency, and a flawed quorum, demanding a fresh, multi-developer ballot. They plan to seek an official report from the district deputy registrar to challenge the decision.

news.title: RERA orders open floodgates for claims against Bengaluru development body
news.para: Bangalore Development Authority (BDA) faces mounting compensation claims from Nadaprabhu Kempegowda Layout (NPKL) plot allottees. Rera has ruled in favour of complainants seeking damages for delayed basic amenities, impacting thousands of plot owners. BDA plans to challenge these orders, but mandatory pre-deposits could pose a financial hurdle. Allottees are seeking compensation for financial losses and a liveable site.

news.title: ED attaches Raheja Developers' assets worth over ₹1,110 crore
news.para: In a significant crackdown, the Enforcement Directorate has frozen assets worth over Rs 1,100 crore belonging to Raheja Developers. This move is part of an ongoing investigation into allegations of fraud aimed at homebuyers. During recent raids, the agency recovered substantial amounts in gold and foreign currency.
